Kickoff instructs agents to cite evidence IDs/anchors (e.g., `EV-001#E1`). - Compiled artifact contains explicit evidence references. --- # Guardrails - Keep the evidence excerpts short and clearly anchored. - Distinguish “quoted/cited” vs `[inference]`. - If a claim lacks evidence, it must be marked as hypothesis/inference. --- # Output - Thread id - Evidence pack summary (IDs + provenance) - Final compiled artifact demonstrating citations ","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:35.251008341-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:35.251008341-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:35.281499706-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:46.405149146-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:51.476457473-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:45:56.547055393-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.13.5","title":"Task: Pilot an evidence-pack-backed session on a non-code topic","description":"# Goal Prove the external evidence channel is usable by running a session on a non-code topic where citations must come from an evidence pack (not model memory). --- # Success criteria - {"id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","title":"Feature: Test→Hypothesis Binding","description":"# Feature: Test→Hypothesis Binding (ENHANCED) ## Purpose Close the research loop by formally connecting test execution to hypothesis state changes. **CRITICAL UPDATES**: Predictions are now first-class entities, and tests MUST have potency checks. ## CRITICAL UPDATES from Review After reviewing the artifact schema and evaluation rubric: ### Issue 1: Predictions Must Be First-Class The artifact schema separates **Predictions Table (Section 3)** from **Discriminative Tests (Section 4)**: - A **Prediction** is: "Under condition X, H1 predicts Y, H2 predicts Z" - A **Test** is: "Do X and observe the result" These are DISTINCT because: - Multiple tests might address the same prediction - A prediction might be tested multiple times with different methods ### Issue 2: Potency Checks Are MANDATORY From evaluation rubric "Potency Check Sufficiency" (0-3): - 0: No potency check included → **AUTOMATIC FAILURE** - 3: Complete potency check: positive control, sensitivity verification, timing validation From artifact schema: "Each test MUST include a potency check" ### Issue 3: Tests Need Evidence-Per-Week Scoring The artifact schema requires tests to have a 12-point score across 4 dimensions: - Likelihood ratio (0-3): How discriminative? - Cost (0-3): How expensive? - Speed (0-3): How fast? - Ambiguity (0-3): How clear is the readout? ### Issue 4: Object Transposition From evaluation rubric "Object Transposition Considered" (0-2): - Has the contributor considered alternative experimental systems? ## Enhanced Schemas ### Prediction Schema (NEW - First-Class Entity) ```typescript export const PredictionSchema = z.object({ /** Stable ID: P-{session_id}-{sequence} */ id: z.string().regex(/^P-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/), /** The observable condition */ condition: z.string().min(10), /** What each hypothesis predicts for this condition */ hypothesisPredictions: z.array(z.object({ hypothesisId: z.string(), prediction: z.string(), rationale: z.string().optional(), })).min(2), // Must discriminate at least 2 hypotheses /** Session where prediction was formulated */ sessionId: z.string(), /** Which tests address this prediction? */ linkedTests: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // T-xxx IDs createdAt: z.string().datetime(), }); ``` ### Enhanced Test Schema ```typescript export const PotencyCheckSchema = z.object({ /** Positive control that would show the effect if present */ positiveControl: z.string(), /** How do we verify detection threshold is adequate? */ sensitivityVerification: z.string().optional(), /** How do we know the assay window is correct? */ timingValidation: z.string().optional(), }); export const EvidencePerWeekScoreSchema = z.object({ likelihoodRatio: z.number().min(0).max(3), // 0=<2:1, 1=2-10:1, 2=10-100:1, 3=>100:1 cost: z.number().min(0).max(3), // 0=>$100K, 1=$10K-$100K, 2=$1K-$10K, 3=<$1K speed: z.number().min(0).max(3), // 0=>1yr, 1=1-6mo, 2=1wk-1mo, 3=<1wk ambiguity: z.number().min(0).max(3), // 0=many confounds, 3=digital readout total: z.number().min(0).max(12), }); export const TestRecordSchema = z.object({ /** Stable ID: T-{session_id}-{sequence} */ id: z.string().regex(/^T-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/), /** Test name for display */ name: z.string(), /** Full procedure description */ procedure: z.string().min(20), /** Which hypotheses does this test discriminate? */ discriminates: z.array(z.string()).min(2), // H-xxx IDs /** Expected outcomes per hypothesis */ expectedOutcomes: z.array(z.object({ hypothesisId: z.string(), outcome: z.string(), })), /** Which predictions does this test address? */ addressesPredictions: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // P-xxx IDs /** MANDATORY: Potency check */ potencyCheck: PotencyCheckSchema, // NOT optional! /** 4-dimension scoring */ evidencePerWeekScore: EvidencePerWeekScoreSchema, /** Object transposition notes (alternative experimental systems) */ objectTransposition: z.object({ considered: z.boolean(), alternatives: z.array(z.object({ system: z.string(), pros: z.string(), cons: z.string(), })).optional(), chosenRationale: z.string().optional(), }).optional(), /** Feasibility assessment */ feasibility: z.object({ requirements: z.string(), difficulty: z.enum(['easy', 'moderate', 'hard', 'very_hard']), blockers: z.array(z.string()).optional(), }), /** Which assumptions does this test require? */ requiredAssumptions: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // A-xxx IDs /** Session where test was designed */ designedInSession: z.string(), /** Test status */ status: z.enum(['designed', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'blocked']), /** If completed, execution record */ execution: TestExecutionSchema.optional(), createdAt: z.string().datetime(), updatedAt: z.string().datetime(), }); ``` ## Validation Rules From evaluation rubric: **Discriminative Power (0-3):** - Test MUST have different expected outcomes for each hypothesis - Outcomes should be binary or quantitatively large differences - Both outcomes must be actually observable **Potency Check Sufficiency (0-3):** - AUTOMATIC FAILURE if no potency check - Must distinguish "no effect" from "assay failed" **Score Calibration Honesty (0-2):** - Scores should be conservative, not all 3s ## CLI Commands ### Prediction Commands (NEW) - `brenner prediction list` - `brenner prediction show ` - `brenner prediction create --condition="..." --h1-predicts="..." --h2-predicts="..."` ### Test Commands (Enhanced) - `brenner test create` — Now REQUIRES potency check, scores - `brenner test validate ` — Check test meets all requirements - `brenner test execute ` — Record execution (unchanged) ## Success Criteria - [ ] Predictions are first-class entities with own schema/storage - [ ] Tests MUST have potency checks (validation error if missing) - [ ] Tests have 4-dimension evidence-per-week scores - [ ] Object transposition tracking available - [ ] Test validation warns on inflated scores - [ ] Links between predictions, tests, and hypotheses ## Related Specs - specs/artifact_schema_v0.1.md — Sections 3, 4 - specs/evaluation_rubric_v0.1.md — Criteria 7, 8, 9, 10","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:41:43.843308752-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:29:03.745723691-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:29:03.745723691-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Prediction schema as first-class entity and TestRecord schema with mandatory potency checks, evidence-per-week scoring, and discriminative power validation. Added validation functions and comprehensive unit tests (74 passing). Fixed validation bugs in createBinaryPrediction and createBinaryTest.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-lm3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:42:04.124513825-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-6n4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:42:04.155992056-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:37:47.395000148-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-mhi","title":"TanStack Store: Create Compare View State Persistence","description":"# Goal Persist the distillation comparison view state so users don't lose their selections. ## Background The CompareView component has UI state that should persist: - leftModel: Which model is in left pane (opus/gpt/gemini) - rightModel: Which model is in right pane - syncScroll: Whether panes scroll together - Per-model scroll positions Currently, refreshing the page resets all this state. ## Store Schema ```typescript interface CompareStore { leftModel: 'opus' | 'gpt' | 'gemini'; rightModel: 'opus' | 'gpt' | 'gemini'; syncScroll: boolean; scrollPositions: Record; // model -> scrollY } ``` ## Implementation ```typescript import { Store } from '@tanstack/store'; const compareStore = new Store({ leftModel: 'opus', rightModel: 'gpt', syncScroll: true, scrollPositions: {}, }); // Persist to localStorage compareStore.subscribe(() => { localStorage.setItem('brenner-compare', JSON.stringify(compareStore.state)); }); // React hook export function useCompareState() { return useStore(compareStore, (s) => s); } export function setLeftModel(model: Model) { compareStore.setState((s) => ({ ...s, leftModel: model })); } ``` ## Integration ```typescript function CompareView() { const { leftModel, rightModel, syncScroll } = useCompareState(); return (
{/* ... */}
); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Model selections persist - [ ] Sync scroll preference persists - [ ] Per-model scroll positions persist - [ ] State restores on page load ## Files to Create - apps/web/src/stores/compareStore.ts - apps/web/src/hooks/useCompareState.ts ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/components/distillation/CompareView.tsx","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:20:13.355992416-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:44:46.446051904-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:44:46.446051904-05:00","close_reason":"Obsolete: Compare view feature was removed in commit 34a5aa6","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mhi","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-59l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:20:36.726561983-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-mqg7","title":"Feature: Operator Intervention Audit Trail","description":"# Goal Implement a formal audit trail for human operator interventions during Brenner Loop sessions, enabling reproducibility, trust verification, and learning from overrides. ## The Gap This Fills Other agents covered: - Human-in-the-loop gates (general concept) - Cockpit runner (orchestration) - Web UI for sessions **What nobody covered:** When a human operator overrides an agent decision (e.g., manually killing a hypothesis, forcing a test to be included, editing an artifact before publish), there is no formal record of: - WHAT was changed - WHO made the change - WHY they made it (rationale) - WHEN it happened relative to agent activities This matters because: 1. **Reproducibility** - Can someone else replicate this research if they dont know a human secretly tweaked the artifact? 2. **Trust** - Was this conclusion reached by agents following the protocol, or did a human push it through? 3. **Learning** - If operators frequently override a certain agent/role, maybe the role prompt needs improvement. 4. **Debugging** - When a session goes wrong, was it agent error or operator error? ## Why This Is Different from Deltas Agent contributions are recorded as DELTA messages with `from: AgentName`. But operator interventions can happen in several ways that bypass the delta flow: 1. **Direct artifact editing** before publish (editing the markdown) 2. **Selective delta exclusion** (compiling without some deltas) 3. **Manual message injection** (posting a DELTA on behalf of an agent) 4. **Override decisions** ("ignore this critique", "force-include this test") 5. **Session control actions** (early termination, forced re-run, role reassignment) ## Proposed Solution ### 1. Operator Intervention Record ```typescript interface OperatorIntervention { id: string; session_id: string; timestamp: string; operator_id: string; // From BRENNER_AGENT_NAME or "human" type: | "artifact_edit" // Direct edit to compiled artifact | "delta_exclusion" // Excluded a delta from compilation | "delta_injection" // Added a delta not from an agent | "decision_override" // Overrode a protocol decision | "session_control" // Terminated, forked, or reset session | "role_reassignment"; // Changed agent-role mappings mid-session target: { message_id?: number; // For delta exclusion/injection artifact_version?: number; item_id?: string; // Hypothesis, test, etc. }; before?: string; // State before intervention (for diffs) after?: string; // State after intervention rationale: string; // Required: WHY did the operator do this? reversible: boolean; reversed_at?: string; reversed_by?: string; } ``` ### 2. Intervention Capture Points | Action | Capture Point | Implementation | |--------|---------------|----------------| | Edit artifact before publish | Compare pre/post in publish action | API route adds intervention record | | Exclude delta | Compile action with exclusion list | Track excluded message_ids | | Manual delta | DELTA message where from = operator | Check if sender matches known agent roster | | Early termination | Session close action | Add "session_control" record | | Role reassignment | Roster change mid-session | Compare before/after roster state | ### 3. Storage and Access **Storage:** Interventions stored as part of session metadata in Agent Mail thread (as INFO: messages with structured payload) or in a separate interventions log. **Access:** - `brenner session interventions ` - List all interventions - Session detail page shows intervention count and expandable list - Compiled artifacts include intervention summary in metadata ### 4. Intervention Severity Levels ``` MINOR: Typo fixes, formatting adjustments MODERATE: Delta exclusion, small edits MAJOR: Killing hypotheses, adding tests, role changes CRITICAL: Session termination, protocol bypass ``` Major/Critical interventions trigger warnings in the UI. ### 5. Reproducibility Mode A "strict" compilation mode that: - Fails if any MAJOR+ interventions exist - Produces a "clean" artifact that is purely agent-derived - Useful for comparing "what would agents have produced alone?" ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `OperatorIntervention` type defined in session types - [ ] Publish action detects and records artifact edits - [ ] Compile action accepts `excludeDeltas` param and records exclusions - [ ] Manual delta detection (sender not in agent roster) - [ ] CLI `brenner session interventions ` command - [ ] Web session detail shows intervention badge/list - [ ] All interventions require `rationale` field - [ ] Compiled artifact metadata includes `intervention_count` ## Dependencies - Agent roster (brenner_bot-5so.12.1.x) - needed to detect "operator vs agent" - Session compile/publish actions (already exist) ## Non-Goals - Not preventing interventions (operators should have full control) - Not implementing approval workflows (thats for future governance) - Not tracking every click/action (only meaningful interventions) ## Background: Why This Matters for Science Real science has lab notebooks that record everything, including mistakes and corrections. The audit trail is the digital equivalent. Without it, a "research result" from a Brenner Loop session cannot be trusted or replicated by others. The Brenner Method emphasizes transparency and honesty about what was done and why. Occams Broom (hiding inconvenient facts) is an anti-pattern. The audit trail ensures operator interventions are visible, not swept under the rug.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:31.405372314-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:43:36.737347608-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mqg7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:38:22.90910216-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-mqg7","title":"Feature: Operator Intervention Audit Trail","description":"# Goal Implement a formal audit trail for human operator interventions during Brenner Loop sessions, enabling reproducibility, trust verification, and learning from overrides. ## The Gap This Fills Other agents covered: - Human-in-the-loop gates (general concept) - Cockpit runner (orchestration) - Web UI for sessions **What nobody covered:** When a human operator overrides an agent decision (e.g., manually killing a hypothesis, forcing a test to be included, editing an artifact before publish), there is no formal record of: - WHAT was changed - WHO made the change - WHY they made it (rationale) - WHEN it happened relative to agent activities This matters because: 1. **Reproducibility** - Can someone else replicate this research if they dont know a human secretly tweaked the artifact? 2. **Trust** - Was this conclusion reached by agents following the protocol, or did a human push it through? 3. **Learning** - If operators frequently override a certain agent/role, maybe the role prompt needs improvement. 4. **Debugging** - When a session goes wrong, was it agent error or operator error? ## Why This Is Different from Deltas Agent contributions are recorded as DELTA messages with `from: AgentName`. But operator interventions can happen in several ways that bypass the delta flow: 1. **Direct artifact editing** before publish (editing the markdown) 2. **Selective delta exclusion** (compiling without some deltas) 3. **Manual message injection** (posting a DELTA on behalf of an agent) 4. **Override decisions** ("ignore this critique", "force-include this test") 5. **Session control actions** (early termination, forced re-run, role reassignment) ## Proposed Solution ### 1. Operator Intervention Record ```typescript interface OperatorIntervention { id: string; session_id: string; timestamp: string; operator_id: string; // From BRENNER_AGENT_NAME or "human" type: | "artifact_edit" // Direct edit to compiled artifact | "delta_exclusion" // Excluded a delta from compilation | "delta_injection" // Added a delta not from an agent | "decision_override" // Overrode a protocol decision | "session_control" // Terminated, forked, or reset session | "role_reassignment"; // Changed agent-role mappings mid-session target: { message_id?: number; // For delta exclusion/injection artifact_version?: number; item_id?: string; // Hypothesis, test, etc. }; before?: string; // State before intervention (for diffs) after?: string; // State after intervention rationale: string; // Required: WHY did the operator do this? reversible: boolean; reversed_at?: string; reversed_by?: string; } ``` ### 2. Intervention Capture Points | Action | Capture Point | Implementation | |--------|---------------|----------------| | Edit artifact before publish | Compare pre/post in publish action | API route adds intervention record | | Exclude delta | Compile action with exclusion list | Track excluded message_ids | | Manual delta | DELTA message where from = operator | Check if sender matches known agent roster | | Early termination | Session close action | Add "session_control" record | | Role reassignment | Roster change mid-session | Compare before/after roster state | ### 3. Storage and Access **Storage:** Interventions stored as part of session metadata in Agent Mail thread (as INFO: messages with structured payload) or in a separate interventions log. **Access:** - `brenner session interventions ` - List all interventions - Session detail page shows intervention count and expandable list - Compiled artifacts include intervention summary in metadata ### 4. Intervention Severity Levels ``` MINOR: Typo fixes, formatting adjustments MODERATE: Delta exclusion, small edits MAJOR: Killing hypotheses, adding tests, role changes CRITICAL: Session termination, protocol bypass ``` Major/Critical interventions trigger warnings in the UI. ### 5. Reproducibility Mode A "strict" compilation mode that: - Fails if any MAJOR+ interventions exist - Produces a "clean" artifact that is purely agent-derived - Useful for comparing "what would agents have produced alone?" ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `OperatorIntervention` type defined in session types - [ ] Publish action detects and records artifact edits - [ ] Compile action accepts `excludeDeltas` param and records exclusions - [ ] Manual delta detection (sender not in agent roster) - [ ] CLI `brenner session interventions ` command - [ ] Web session detail shows intervention badge/list - [ ] All interventions require `rationale` field - [ ] Compiled artifact metadata includes `intervention_count` ## Dependencies - Agent roster (brenner_bot-5so.12.1.x) - needed to detect "operator vs agent" - Session compile/publish actions (already exist) ## Non-Goals - Not preventing interventions (operators should have full control) - Not implementing approval workflows (thats for future governance) - Not tracking every click/action (only meaningful interventions) ## Background: Why This Matters for Science Real science has lab notebooks that record everything, including mistakes and corrections. The audit trail is the digital equivalent. Without it, a "research result" from a Brenner Loop session cannot be trusted or replicated by others. The Brenner Method emphasizes transparency and honesty about what was done and why. Occams Broom (hiding inconvenient facts) is an anti-pattern. The audit trail ensures operator interventions are visible, not swept under the rug.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"GoldFalcon","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:31.405372314-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T15:02:15.667567682-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mqg7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:38:22.90910216-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-mth","title":"Task: Unit tests for lib/parsers.ts","description":"Write comprehensive unit tests for document parsers. ## Functions to Test (need to verify file exists first) - Markdown parsing - Section extraction - Anchor parsing - Metadata extraction ## Test Fixtures - Use real corpus documents as fixtures - Include edge cases: empty, malformed, large files ## Test Cases - Valid markdown parsing - Section boundary detection - Anchor/citation extraction (§n format) - Error handling for invalid input - Performance with large documents","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:34.204022025-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:56:07.573038512-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T22:56:07.573038512-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: Added 109 comprehensive unit tests for all parsers (transcript, distillation, quotebank, metaprompt)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mth","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0b5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:57.497567726-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mth","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-l8y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:59.317778324-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mth","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-hnn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:32.676627321-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-nihv","title":"Feature: Brenner Method Scorecard","description":"# Feature: Brenner Method Scorecard (COMPREHENSIVE REWRITE) ## Purpose Implement the evaluation rubric as automated tooling. Provides per-session and per-contribution scores on Brenner method adherence with actionable feedback. ## CRITICAL REWRITE The original 7-dimension design was **too simplified**. The evaluation rubric has **14 specific criteria** organized by role. The scorecard must align with the actual rubric. ## Rubric Alignment ### Universal Criteria (All Contributions) | # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures | |---|-----------|--------|------------------| | 1 | Structural Correctness | 0-3 | Delta format compliance | | 2 | Citation Compliance | 0-3 | §n anchors, [inference] markers | | 3 | Rationale Quality | 0-3 | Explains why, which operators | ### Hypothesis Generator (Codex) Criteria | # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures | |---|-----------|--------|------------------| | 4 | Level Separation | 0-3 | ⊘ operator, no conflation | | 5 | Third Alternative Presence | 0-3 | Genuine third option | | 6 | Paradox Exploitation | 0-2 | Uses anomalies productively | ### Test Designer (Opus) Criteria | # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures | |---|-----------|--------|------------------| | 7 | Discriminative Power | 0-3 | Tests distinguish hypotheses | | 8 | Potency Check Sufficiency | 0-3 | Can detect assay failure | | 9 | Object Transposition | 0-2 | Alternative systems considered | | 10 | Score Calibration Honesty | 0-2 | Conservative, not inflated | ### Adversarial Critic (Gemini) Criteria | # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures | |---|-----------|--------|------------------| | 11 | Scale Check Rigor | 0-3 | Physics calculations, not hand-waves | | 12 | Anomaly Quarantine Discipline | 0-3 | Tracking, not sweeping | | 13 | Theory Kill Justification | 0-3 | Kills are evidenced | | 14 | Real Third Alternative | 0-3 | Constructive, not just skeptical | ## Data Sources Now that we have all registries, each dimension has a proper data source: | Criterion | Data Source | |-----------|-------------| | Citation Compliance | Hypothesis.anchors, Test.anchors (from registries) | | Level Separation | Hypothesis.mechanism validation | | Third Alternative Presence | Count of hypotheses with category='third_alternative' | | Paradox Exploitation | Anomaly Registry: anomalies that spawned hypotheses | | Discriminative Power | Test predictions: are outcomes different? | | Potency Check Sufficiency | Test.potencyCheck presence and completeness | | Scale Check Rigor | Assumption Registry: count and quality of scale_physics assumptions | | Anomaly Quarantine Discipline | Anomaly Registry: status tracking | | Theory Kill Justification | Critique→Kill linkage in transitions | | Real Third Alternative | Critique.proposedAlternative with testable=true | ## Per-Contribution Scoring From rubric, weighted composite per role: **Hypothesis Generator (Codex):** ``` Score = (Structural × 1.0) + (Citation × 1.0) + (Rationale × 0.5) + (Level-Sep × 1.5) + (Third-Alt × 2.0) + (Paradox × 0.5) Max = 19 points ``` **Test Designer (Opus):** ``` Score = (Structural × 1.0) + (Citation × 1.0) + (Rationale × 0.5) + (Discriminative × 2.0) + (Potency × 2.0) + (Object-Trans × 0.5) + (Calibration × 0.5) Max = 21.5 points ``` **Adversarial Critic (Gemini):** ``` Score = (Structural × 1.0) + (Citation × 1.0) + (Rationale × 0.5) + (Scale × 1.5) + (Quarantine × 1.5) + [Kill-Justify × 1.5]* + (Real-Third × 1.5) Max (with KILL) = 25.5 points Max (ADD/EDIT) = 21 points ``` ## Session-Level Aggregation For evaluating a complete session: 1. **Volume**: Count of valid contributions per role 2. **Quality**: Mean composite score per role 3. **Progression**: Did quality improve over rounds? 4. **Convergence**: Did hypotheses narrow (kills > adds by end)? 5. **Coverage**: Were all operators in the role's toolkit used? ## Pass/Fail Gates Certain failures are automatic disqualifiers: | Failure | Consequence | |---------|-------------| | Invalid JSON in delta | Delta rejected | | Missing potency check | Test rejected | | Fake transcript anchor | Contribution flagged | | KILL without rationale | KILL rejected | | Third alternative missing | Hypothesis set incomplete | ## Warning Thresholds | Condition | Warning | |-----------|---------| | Composite < 50% of max | Low-quality contribution | | > 3 ADDs without KILL | Possible hypothesis sprawl | | Scale check missing for mechanism claim | Unverified claim | | Potency score < 2 | Weak assay design | ## Feedback Generation For each low-scoring dimension, generate specific feedback: **Example for Level Separation (scored 1/3):** ``` [WARNING] Level Separation (1/3) Issue: Hypothesis mechanism shows level conflation Found: "The gene tells the cell to differentiate" (H-RS20251230-001) Problem: This anthropomorphizes genes with agency Suggestion: Reframe as mechanism: "Gene X expression enables transcription of differentiation factors" Sydney Brenner says: "Programs don't have wants. Interpreters do." (§58) ``` ## CLI Commands - `brenner score ` — Score session with all 14 criteria - `brenner score contribution ` — Score single contribution - `brenner feedback ` — Detailed feedback per dimension - `brenner validate ` — Check pass/fail gates only - `brenner score --program=` — Aggregate across program sessions ## Integration Points - **Hypothesis Registry**: Validates mechanism, anchors, category - **Assumption Registry**: Validates scale_physics presence/quality - **Anomaly Registry**: Validates quarantine discipline - **Critique Registry**: Validates kill justification - **Test Binding**: Validates discriminative power, potency ## Success Criteria - [ ] All 14 criteria from rubric implemented - [ ] Per-role weighted composites match rubric - [ ] Data sources connected to registries - [ ] Pass/fail gates enforced - [ ] Warning thresholds generate alerts - [ ] Brenner quotes integrated into feedback - [ ] Session-level aggregation working ## Related Specs - specs/evaluation_rubric_v0.1.md — The authoritative source","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:41:45.805584341-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T02:54:12.920863516-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T02:54:12.920863516-05:00","close_reason":"Scorecard schema implemented with all 14 criteria, scoring functions, pass/fail gates, warning thresholds, and Brenner quotes. 49 tests passing. Subtask nihv.1 (registry integration) remains for future implementation.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-6n4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:42:04.186796721-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:42:04.219302717-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5kr7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:05:55.568354717-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-vxd3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:05:55.600017521-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f5wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:05:55.630890879-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:38:07.686497867-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-nihv.1","title":"Integrate Registries with Scorecard Data Sources","description":"Connect Assumption, Anomaly, and Critique registries to the Scorecard.
